Additional Tips	1

-- 2 of 13 --

THREE KEY ELEMENTS OF A
GOOD SCRIPT
Now that you understand the basic structure of a script, let’s talk about the
some of the textures and nuances that make Straight Line scripts special.
Those elements are:
EFFECTIVE BODY LANGUAGE
EFFECTIVE TONALITY
EFFECTIVE LANGUAGE PATTERNS
By using the three key elements of a good script, you allow yourself the
opportunity to say only what you should be saying and to say it with the
correct tonality and gestures, whether you’re in person or on the phone.
>*'+#I8-/'&9#+-/.+#7.*-5'"%#7.(.4&+#("&#"(/9#&*.#'(0'5'08-/#+-/.+$.%+"(2#
but also your entire salesforce. It’s one of the key differences between
working hard and working smart.
1
2
3
JORDAN BELFORT
Additional Tips	2

-- 3 of 13 --

IN PERSON SALES
When you’re performing a sales call in person, you simply cannot have a
+3%'$&#'(#)%"(&#")#9"86##?"8@%.#"(/9#"$&'"(#'+#&"#,.,"%'C.#'&#3"/0a##?"8#0"(@&#
want to be searching for something intelligent to say when you’re in front
of your client; you want to know what you should be saying and when.
Equally important to the way you deliver your presentation, is the way you
present yourself while doing so.
Effective Body Language
Body language is more than just the way you stand or the
gestures that you use; it is the management of the space around
you and the way you move through that space as you enter a
room or greet a potential client.
Many salespeople overlook the importance of body language as a
selling skill, but it is an integral part of the Straight Line System.
Something as simple as where you position yourself in relation
to the client during your sales presentation can make a huge
difference in your potential not just to close, but also to have your
presentation heard.
For example, if you’re a man selling to a woman, you should
stand directly in front of her, not off to the side, with a couple of
feet between you so that she can see your hands. If you invade
a woman’s space, she will immediately withdraw. If you’re a man
selling to a man, the situation is reversed. Standing directly in
front of a man will be taken as adversarial, thus making your male
client uncomfortable and perhaps too distracted to close.
1
JORDAN BELFORT
Additional Tips	3

-- 4 of 13 --

Direct eye contact is another simple piece of body language
that can affect your sales. You should make direct eye contact
approximately 72% of the time. Less than that and the client’s
&%8+&#'(#9"8#0%"$+#+'1('43-(&/96##H-;'(1#+&%"(1#.9.#3"(&-3&#78'/0+#
&%8+&#-(0#$"+'&'"(+#9"8#-+#-(#-8&*"%'&9#418%.6
Even some odd details such as the rings you wear (pinky rings
have been shown to dramatically reduce a person’s closing ratio)
and your facial hair (goatees have been shown to be a problem
unless well trimmed and conservative) can have an effect on your
overall sale.
Though you don’t always know what factors will help or hinder
your sales, managing the items you can control will only help to
better your chances of closing!
JORDAN BELFORT
Additional Tips	4

-- 5 of 13 --

THE IMPORTANCE OF
MATCHING AND MIRRORING
Matching and mirroring enables you to create
the appropriate emotion that allows the client to make
the right decision—to buy your product.
That’s the power of body language!
Matching and Mirroring
Another advantage to inperson sales is your ability to use matching and
mirroring. This tool uses body language to develop massive rapport with
another person. It doesn’t mean doing exactly what your client does right
after they do it, but rather waiting a few moments and then mirroring their
stance or positioning.
For example, if someone is sitting with their legs crossed and you
sit down to talk with them, wait a few seconds, then cross your legs
and settle into the same posture in the chair. This is a very powerful
technique for gaining rapport. It operates on an unconscious level,
but never underestimate its power to build rapport and connect with
another person.
Matching and mirroring is all about getting into the other person’s world so
that you can lead them through the sales process. You enter at the same
level, and then slowly move that level up or down depending on where
you are in the presentation or close.
JORDAN BELFORT
Additional Tips	5

-- 6 of 13 --

Effective Tonality
Tonality is important no matter what type of sales you are
performing. There are certain gestures that accompany your
tonality and you need to be aware of these gestures in
persontoperson sales. Those gestures are simple movements
9"8#,-;.#='&*#9"8%#7"09\7.#'&#-#3/-$#")#9"8%#*-(0+2#-#4+&#$8,$2#
-(#-'%#*'1*G45.2#=*-&.5.%#&*.#3-+.#,-9#7.\&"#1.&#9"8#'(#+",.#
&9$.#")#$*9+'3-/#-(0#.,"&'"(-/#+&-&.#")#3"(40.(3.2#3.%&-'(&92#
happiness, or something else. If you can learn how to link certain
gestures to your tonalities, it will help you to put the punch in your
script in different areas where you need to summon that state of
3.%&-'(&9#"%#3"(40.(3.#&"#*./$#='&*#9"8%#+-/.6
# 	# 	#P"=#9"8#8+.#9"8%#5"'3.#&"#'(D8.(3.#-#+-/.#'+#-#;.9#3",$"(.(&#
to becoming an outstanding salesperson. In a sales situation,
tonality goes handinhand with information gathering which goes
handinhand with building rapport.
Building Rapport with Questions and Tonality
How you ask a question will elicit completely different answers and
emotional responses.
To achieve rapport, every tonality must be precisely
engineered—not in a deceptive way, but in a way that
empowers the client to answer gracefully when you ask a
question. Asking questions with the right tonality triggers a
response that allows you to gather maximum intelligence
while maintaining rapport with the client as you move down
the line to complete the sale.
2
JORDAN BELFORT
Additional Tips	6

-- 7 of 13 --

For example, you don’t want to begin a sale by asking a
moneyrelated question. Instead, ask a series of less probing
questions that get the client in the habit of saying, “yes,” and
offering information.
Once you have established deeper rapport with your client, you
can ask, “Oh, by the way, what do you have in the market? How
much do you owe on the mortgage right now?” or “How much are
you behind right now? Just a ballpark.”
Tonality—what you ask, how you ask it, and when you ask it—is
your primary tool for moving down the Straight Line. You must
master this skill to achieve a high level of success.
# 	# 	#>*.#&"(-/'&9#9"8#8+.#=*.(#-+;'(1#9"8%#7'1#$'3&8%.#-(0#+$.3'43#
questions will effect how your client perceives you as well as how
they respond to you. Remember, the primary goal of building
rapport is to gather massive amounts of intelligence about your
client in a way that neither offends nor annoys them, but rather
empowers them, and in turn, you.
For example, suppose your client objects to the amount of money
you’re proposing. Do not respond with, “Okay, but money aside,
do you like the idea?” because the client knows that money is part
of the equation and asking them to put it aside positions you as
an adversary rather than a friend. Your “money aside” comment
will be perceived as a sales “trick” that the client will see through
almost instantaneously.
More often than not, sales is about being mellow and
persuasive through the use of tonalities that are pleasing to
the ear and that insert subliminal messages as you’re talking.
JORDAN BELFORT
Additional Tips	7

-- 8 of 13 --

TONALITY + BODY LANGUAGE = RAPPORT!
Instead, in a friendly tone, ask your client, “Do you like the idea?
Does it make sense to you?” This tonality implies “money aside”
without actually saying it, thus keeping you in rapport with your
client. If it turns out that money is a real issue rather than just a
standard objection, keeping rapport will give you the opportunity to
4(0#"&*.%#=-9+#&"#,-;.#9"8%#$%"083&#-))"%0-7/.#&"#&*.#3/'.(&6
Remember, rapport is comprised of two main components:
>"(-/'&9#-(0#7"09#/-(18-1.6##:+.#I8.+&'"(+#='&*#&*.#%'1*&#&"(-/'&9#
and add the distinctions you learned about body language and you
will be unstoppable!
PHONE SALES
Selling over the phone involves using similar components of
facetoface sales, but tonality reigns supreme. You have to
accomplish the same goals of inperson sales without the use of
body language, gestures, or appearance. Paying special attention
to pitch, energy level, and tone of voice are key as those are
components you must manage to help the client create a positive
picture of you, your company, and your product in their mind’s eye.
With more and more sales taking place by phone, using the proper
tonality is an increasingly important skill for salespeople.
Remember, in the absence of physical communication, only 9%
of your impact comes from your words, while 91% comes from
your tone.
JORDAN BELFORT
Additional Tips	8

-- 9 of 13 --

Effective Language Patterns
The third element, effective language patterns, affects any sale
that you make, be it in person or on the phone. A language
pattern is a chunk of words, perhaps one to two paragraphs, that
*-+#-#0.4(.0#7.1'(('(1#-(0#-#0.4(.0#.(0'(12#='&*#-#+$.3'43#
outcome attached.
You can have different language patterns attached to your script
&*-&#-%.#0.+'1(.0#+$.3'43-//9#&"#'(3%.-+.#3.%&-'(&9#'(#9"8%#$%"083&2#
sell your company, or sell yourself. How you write these language
patterns are up to each particular salesperson, especially the
selfselling patterns. However, the language patterns regarding
the product and the company can be tailored for a company’s
entire salesforce.
Language patterns can be used to decrease someone’s action
threshold or elevate another’s pain threshold. Just remember
to be ethical and empathetic. Don’t elevate someone’s pain
threshold if you’re not ready to offer him an actual solution. In this
instance, you can show a potential client how you, your product or
service, and/or your company can take them from the pain they’re
currently feeling to a place where they can attain their goals and
vision for their life.
3
JORDAN BELFORT
Additional Tips	9

-- 10 of 13 --

Why You Need to Build Rapport
Sales is an intensely personal business! Your success depends on your
-7'/'&9#&"#'(&.%-3&#='&*#"&*.%+#-(0#'(D8.(3.#&*.,#'(#-#$"+'&'5.#,-((.%6##
That’s why a large part of the Straight Line System focuses on teaching
you how to build rapport through these three elements—body language,
tonality, and language patterns. The rapport you build is not related solely
to a client liking you—they won’t buy from you simply because they like
you—they must see you as a highly competent professional who knows
the product inside and out and can passionately communicate the value
of that product. The best sales closers are those who achieve these
things by building rapport with each and every client.
And what’s the best way to keep your rapport with your client will still
closing the sale? 	To follow your script from the Straight Line system.
JORDAN BELFORT
Additional Tips	10

-- 11 of 13 --

KEY ELEMENTS OF
SCRIPT WRITING: A REVIEW
Remember, in the beginning, a script is a work in progress.
Use correct structure.
There are three main sections of a script: The Introduction or
Opening, the Body or Presentation, and the Close. But, you must
.(+8%.#&*-&#&*.#D"=#7.&=..(#&*.+.#&*%..#+.3&'"(+#'+#+.-,/.++6##
One way to do this is to create “stopping off points” between
sections where your transitions fall.
Some questions to ask yourself while writing: a) Is it effective? Is
it going to convert a sale? b) Is it easy for someone to use? Is it
able to be duplicated?
Use correct sound.
Your script should not sound like a script. It should sound natural
and pleasing to the ear. One of the ways to make this happen
is to use colloquialisms within your writing. See the highlighted
phrases within the script paragraph below for examples.
Okay, great! Well, getting you those customers is going to be very
simple. It’s just a question of some very basic information to get
your website [updated and/or 341-$!1*3$$%$5] and then you list
the towns you want more business in and we’ll launch your online
campaign. 6+34,&+,$3&/7 the whole thing takes less than two
days, and we can have your phone *%$5%$51'%8"10*-9(:
1Each word is crucial! Paint a picture of your product, service,
company, or the client’s bright future after buying your product
1
2
3
JORDAN BELFORT
Additional Tips	11

-- 12 of 13 --

4
5
6
7
using comparisons and metaphors. Then, reread your script 100
&',.+6##K%-3&'3.#'&#"8&#/"802#/"";'(1#)"%#&"(18.G&='+&.%+2#$""%#D"=2#
or anything else that just doesn’t sound right. A script may read
well, but that doesn’t mean it sounds natural when spoken. Plus,
the more you practice it the easier it will roll off your tongue.
Use correct balance.
Pay very close attention to the details of your script, the syllables,
beats, and pauses in each sentence.
# 	# 	#>*.#4%+&#45.#="%0+#9"8#+-9#3-8+.#-#$"&.(&'-/#3/'.(&#&"#,-;.#
a mental picture of you—be it boring or enthusiastic, dull or
passionate, expert or idiot.
Do not frontload your script.
Save your best components for “after the fact!”
Be honest and ethical.
Come from a place of integrity. Be accurate—do not use lies or
wild exaggerations.
Keep your script interactive.
Don’t just preach to them or talk at them.
Create urgency in the last paragraph.
Go into a soft close with the ending, “Fair enough?”
! 	! 	! 	!CD+-/'!5,3*!1*#'!E%,4F!!C:G*)/-!$'!$%'-*%)..5!
and tonally.
8

JORDAN BELFORT
